name: Belgian Spice Crisps
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
2. Add the corn syrup, lemon rind, juice, rum, and spices, then beat well.
3. Gradually stir in the flour until a dough forms a ball. (The dough can be prepared in a food processor using the same procedure.)
4. If the flour has been packed closely, add 1-2 teaspoons of water to correct proportions.
5. Divide the dough into two pieces and pat each into a rectangular shape.
6. Chill for 1-2 hours in the refrigerator or freeze for 15 minutes.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Measure aluminum foil large enough for a cookie sheet, shiny side down.
9. Place one rectangle of dough on the foil and gently roll with a rolling pin to soften slightly.
10. Cover with wax paper the same length as the foil and secure with straight pins.
11. Roll the dough between the foil and wax paper until very thin and crispy. Press firmly; avoid working too fast.
12. Lift the dough onto the cookie sheet, foil side down. Remove pins and carefully peel off wax paper.
13. Cut into desired shapes (squares, rectangles, diamonds, triangles) through the dough to prevent breakage later.
14. Bake for about 10 minutes or until lightly browned; edges brown faster.
15. Repeat with remaining dough.
16. Store in an airtight container. Note: Each crisp contains approximately 23 calories.
